message:
feat(wezterm): kill_pane_with_cx + zoom_pane_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Follow-on to tick 40's `run_cli_with_cx` /
`run_cli_with_pane_check_with_cx` helpers. Migrate the two remaining
pane-lifecycle subprocess helpers to Cx-first entry points — now
that the helper chain is landed, each concrete method is a trivial
10-line parallel to the legacy version.

  * `WeztermClient::kill_pane_with_cx(cx, pane_id)` — direct Cx
    parallel of `kill_pane`. Threads `cx` through
    `run_cli_with_pane_check_with_cx` so `wezterm cli kill-pane`
    honors caller cancellation/budget/virtual time.

  * `WeztermClient::zoom_pane_with_cx(cx, pane_id, zoom)` — Cx
    parallel of `zoom_pane`. Preserves the identical `--unzoom` arg
    construction logic for `zoom=false` so the Cx and legacy paths
    build identical argv.

Both are `#[cfg(feature = "asupersync-runtime")]`-gated and strictly
additive. The non-Cx `kill_pane` / `zoom_pane` methods remain
untouched; non-migrated callers continue to route through the legacy
`timeout()` path unchanged.

Regression guards — two new short-circuit tests mirroring tick 40's
activate_pane pattern:

  * `kill_pane_with_cx_short_circuits_on_missing_socket` — ensures
    `SocketNotFound` surfaces at the pre-check on the Cx path.

  * `zoom_pane_with_cx_short_circuits_on_missing_socket` — exercises
    BOTH `zoom=true` (no extra arg) and `zoom=false` (appends
    `--unzoom`) branches, with separate bogus-socket assertions each.
    Catches regressions in arg construction on the Cx path (e.g. if a
    refactor swapped the branch direction, the non-short-circuit
    subprocess error would not match `SocketNotFound` and the test
    would fail loudly).

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * wezterm:: — 163 passed / 0 failed (tick 40: 161 -> tick 41: 163,
    +2 new tests, all prior tests still green)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Pane-lifecycle Cx-first surface now complete on WeztermClient
inherent methods: `activate_pane`, `kill_pane`, `zoom_pane` all have
`_with_cx` entry points going through the shared helper chain. Next
migration targets on this trail: `spawn`, `spawn_targeted`,
`split_pane`.

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
